behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff (HTTP handler + signature verifier + enqueue path). A pure unit test of only the HMAC function would miss miswiring in the handler. No behavior text requires real network/OS/subprocess, real AI output, or multi-agent steps, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook handler in-process, send a request with an invalid `X-Signature-256`, assert HTTP 401, and assert enqueue/publish dependency was not called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across handler and queue modules. Use a controllable queue implementation (or test double) to verify response is sent only after successful write. Behavior text does not require real HTTP/OS effects or external systems, so System is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke handler in-process with a queue write promise you can gate; assert 200 is not returned before write resolves, then assert 200 after write completion.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure is orchestration between enqueue/dispatch and HTTP response lifecycle. An integration test is needed to observe non-blocking behavior across modules. No real OS/network primitive is required by the behavior text, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process handler with downstream processor intentionally blocked/delayed; assert response is returned promptly while processor is still running.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a wall-clock latency/concurrency SLO under concurrent traffic; real HTTP scheduling and runtime behavior can expose failures hidden by in-process fakes. Behavior text explicitly requires wall-clock timing under concurrency, which justifies System.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service, create concurrent load where earlier events trigger slow downstream work, send a new webhook via real HTTP, and assert end-to-end response time is under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Deduplication is a stateful cross-module behavior (handler + persistence/queue write path). A single-function unit test may miss persistence interaction. Behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Send two requests with the same event ID in-process; assert first is written and second is not (or is no-op), and queue contains one record for that ID.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This is error mapping across queue module and HTTP layer. A mock/stub queue-full signal is sufficient to catch real mapping failures. Behavior text does not require real OS/network conditions beyond in-process module wiring, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue dependency to return `full` condition; call handler and assert status 503 and presence/value format of `Retry-After` header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates real queue implementation with its persistence layer/config wiring, not just handler logic. A mock would hide true capacity-enforcement bugs. No behavior text requires real external network/subprocess; local DB/filesystem-backed integration is sufficient, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize queue with small configured capacity in test persistence backend, enqueue up to limit, then attempt one more and assert rejection/error indicating capacity exceeded.
